API docs by Redocly

AxonOps Schema Registry (1.0.0)

Download OpenAPI specification:

URL: https://github.com/axonops/axonops-schema-registry License: Apache 2.0

AxonOps Schema Registry is a drop-in replacement for the Confluent Schema Registry, providing full API compatibility with extensions for enterprise security and multi-backend storage. It supports Avro, Protobuf, and JSON Schema formats for schema management.

Key Concepts

  • Schema: A versioned definition of a data structure (Avro record, Protobuf message, or JSON Schema document). Schemas are stored centrally and assigned a globally unique ID.
  • Subject: A named scope under which schema versions are registered. A subject typically maps to a Kafka topic (e.g. my-topic-value).
  • Version: An incrementing integer assigned each time a new schema is registered under a subject.
  • Compatibility: A policy that controls whether a new schema version is allowed based on its relationship to previous versions. Levels include BACKWARD, FORWARD, FULL, and their TRANSITIVE variants.
  • Mode: Controls whether a subject (or the entire registry) accepts writes. Modes include READWRITE, READONLY, READONLY_OVERRIDE, and IMPORT.
  • Reference: A pointer from one schema to another, enabling cross-subject schema composition (e.g. Avro named types, Protobuf imports, JSON Schema $ref).
  • Metadata: Key-value tags and properties attached to a schema for data contract management.
  • RuleSet: Data contract rules (migration and domain rules) attached to a schema.

Content Types

The primary content type is application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json. The registry also accepts application/json.

Error Handling

All errors are returned as JSON objects with error_code and message fields. Error codes follow the Confluent Schema Registry convention (e.g. 40401 for subject not found, 42201 for invalid schema).

Authentication

When security is enabled, the registry supports HTTP Basic authentication, API key authentication (via the X-API-Key header), and JWT bearer tokens. Public endpoints (health check, metrics, documentation) do not require authentication.

Schemas

Operations for retrieving schemas by their globally unique ID, listing all schemas, and querying supported schema types. Every schema registered in the registry receives a unique integer ID that persists across subjects.

Get supported schema types

Returns the list of schema types supported by this registry. The response is an array of strings. Currently supported types are AVRO, PROTOBUF, and JSON.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/types

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "AVRO",
  • "PROTOBUF",
  • "JSON"
]

List schemas

Returns a list of all schemas registered in the registry. Results MAY be filtered by subject prefix, soft-deleted status, and whether to return only the latest version per subject. Pagination is supported via offset and limit query parameters.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
query Parameters
subjectPrefix
string

Filter results to schemas whose subject name starts with this prefix. If omitted, schemas across all subjects are returned.

deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, soft-deleted schemas are included in the results.

latestOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, only the latest version of each subject is returned.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return. If omitted or set to 0, all results are returned.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • {
    • "subject": "string",
    • "version": 0,
    • "id": 0,
    • "schemaType": "AVRO",
    • "schema": "string",
    • "references": [
      • {
        • "name": "com.example.Address",
        • "subject": "address-value",
        • "version": 1
        }
      ],
    • "metadata": {
      • "tags": {
        • "team": [
          • "platform",
          • "data-eng"
          ]
        },
      • "properties": {
        • "owner": "data-platform-team",
        • "classification": "internal"
        },
      • "sensitive": [
        • "ssn",
        • "email"
        ]
      },
    • "ruleSet": {
      • "migrationRules": [
        • {
          • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
          • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
          • "kind": "CONDITION",
          • "mode": "WRITE",
          • "type": "CEL",
          • "tags": [
            • "string"
            ],
          • "params": {
            • "property1": "string",
            • "property2": "string"
            },
          • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
          • "onSuccess": "string",
          • "onFailure": "string",
          • "disabled": false,
          • "enableAt": 0
          }
        ],
      • "domainRules": [
        • {
          • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
          • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
          • "kind": "CONDITION",
          • "mode": "WRITE",
          • "type": "CEL",
          • "tags": [
            • "string"
            ],
          • "params": {
            • "property1": "string",
            • "property2": "string"
            },
          • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
          • "onSuccess": "string",
          • "onFailure": "string",
          • "disabled": false,
          • "enableAt": 0
          }
        ],
      • "encodingRules": [
        • {
          • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
          • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
          • "kind": "CONDITION",
          • "mode": "WRITE",
          • "type": "CEL",
          • "tags": [
            • "string"
            ],
          • "params": {
            • "property1": "string",
            • "property2": "string"
            },
          • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
          • "onSuccess": "string",
          • "onFailure": "string",
          • "disabled": false,
          • "enableAt": 0
          }
        ]
      }
    }
]

Get schema by global ID

Retrieves a schema by its globally unique ID. The response includes the schema string, its type, any references, metadata, and ruleset. The optional format query parameter allows requesting the schema in an alternative serialization (e.g. serialized for Protobuf's normalized format). When fetchMaxId is set to true, the response includes the current maximum schema ID in the registry. The subject query parameter MAY be used as a hint but does not filter results.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The globally unique integer ID of the schema.

query Parameters
format
string

An optional format hint for the returned schema string. For Protobuf schemas, passing serialized returns the normalized descriptor representation.

fetchMaxId
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes a maxId field containing the current highest schema ID in the registry.

subject
string

An optional subject name filter. This parameter is accepted for Confluent API compatibility.

referenceFormat
string
Value: "RESOLVED"

Controls how schema references are returned. When set to RESOLVED, each reference in the response includes inline schema content resolved from the registry. Default behavior returns references without inline schema content.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/ids/{id}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",

    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",

          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {

        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"

Get raw schema string by global ID

Retrieves the raw schema string for the schema identified by its globally unique ID. Unlike GET /schemas/ids/{id}, this endpoint returns only the schema content as a string, without metadata or references. The optional format query parameter allows requesting an alternative serialization.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The globally unique integer ID of the schema.

query Parameters
format
string

An optional format hint for the returned schema string.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/ids/{id}/schema

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
"string"

Get subjects associated with a schema ID

Returns the list of subject names that have registered the schema identified by the given global ID. This is useful for determining which subjects share the same schema content. Soft-deleted subjects MAY be included by setting deleted=true. An optional subject query parameter filters the results to a specific subject name.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The globally unique integer ID of the schema.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes subjects that have been soft-deleted.

subject
string

An optional subject name to filter the result to only that subject.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/ids/{id}/subjects

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-topic-value",
  • "other-topic-value"
]

Get subject-version pairs for a schema ID

Returns all subject-version pairs where the schema identified by the given global ID is registered. A single schema MAY be registered under multiple subjects at different version numbers. Soft-deleted versions MAY be included by setting deleted=true. An optional subject filter restricts results to a specific subject name.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The globally unique integer ID of the schema.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes soft-deleted subject-version pairs.

subject
string

An optional subject name to filter results to only versions under that subject.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/ids/{id}/versions

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • {
    • "subject": "my-topic-value",
    • "version": 1
    }
]

[Context-scoped] Get supported schema types

Context-scoped version of /schemas/types. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/schemas/types

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "AVRO",
  • "PROTOBUF",
  • "JSON"
]

fetchMaxId

subject

An optional subject name filter.

referenceFormat
string
Value: "RESOLVED"

Controls how schema references are returned. When set to RESOLVED, each reference in the response includes inline schema content resolved from the registry. Default behavior returns references without inline schema content.

deleted

Subjects

Operations for managing subjects and their schema versions. A subject is a named scope (typically corresponding to a Kafka topic) under which one or more schema versions are registered. Subjects support soft-delete and permanent-delete semantics.

List subjects

Returns a list of all registered subject names. By default only active (non-deleted) subjects are returned. Use the deleted query parameter to include soft-deleted subjects, or deletedOnly to return only soft-deleted subjects. Results MAY be filtered by a subject name prefix and paginated with offset and limit.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes soft-deleted subjects alongside active ones.

deletedOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns only subjects that have been soft-deleted. This implicitly sets deleted=true.

subjectPrefix
string

Filters the results to subjects whose name starts with the given prefix.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return. If omitted, all results are returned.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-topic-value",
  • "other-topic-key"
]

List versions under a subject

Returns a list of version numbers registered under the specified subject. By default only active (non-deleted) versions are returned. Use deleted=true to include soft-deleted versions, or deletedOnly=true to return exclusively soft-deleted versions. Pagination is supported via offset and limit.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes soft-deleted versions alongside active ones.

deletedOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns only versions that have been soft-deleted. This implicitly sets deleted=true.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return. If omitted, all versions are returned.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/versions

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • 1,
  • 2,
  • 3
]

Register a new schema under a subject

Registers a new schema version under the specified subject. If an identical schema (by content fingerprint) already exists under this subject, the existing schema's ID is returned without creating a new version. The schema MUST be compatible with previous versions according to the subject's compatibility policy, unless the compatibility level is set to NONE. The request body MUST include the schema field containing the schema definition as a string. The schemaType field defaults to AVRO if omitted. References to other schemas MAY be included via the references array. When the normalize query parameter is set to true, the schema string is canonicalized before fingerprinting and storage. If the request includes an explicit id field and the registry is in IMPORT mode, the schema is imported with that specific ID. The subject's mode MUST be READWRITE or IMPORT for this operation to succeed. If the subject is in READONLY or READONLY_OVERRIDE mode, a 42205 error is returned.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the schema is canonicalized (normalized) before storage and fingerprinting. This ensures semantically equivalent schemas produce the same fingerprint.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The schema definition as a string. For Avro, this is a JSON string. For Protobuf, this is the .proto file content. For JSON Schema, this is a JSON Schema document as a string.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that this schema depends on.

id
integer <int64>

An explicit schema ID to assign. This is used in IMPORT mode for migrating schemas while preserving their original IDs.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/versions

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "{\"type\":\"record\",\"name\":\"User\",\"fields\":[{\"name\":\"name\",\"type\":\"string\"}]}",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ],
  • "id": 0,
  • "metadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },
    • "properties": {
      • "owner": "data-platform-team",
      • "classification": "internal"
      },
    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "ruleSet": {
    • "migrationRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ]
    }
}

Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "id": 1
}

Get a specific version of a subject

Retrieves the schema registered under the given subject at the specified version number. The version path parameter accepts an integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to retrieve the most recently registered version. When deleted=true is set, soft-deleted versions are also returned. The optional format query parameter allows requesting an alternative schema serialization.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, soft-deleted versions are also retrievable.

format
string

An optional format hint for the returned schema string.

referenceFormat
string
Value: "RESOLVED"

Controls how schema references are returned. When set to RESOLVED, each reference in the response includes inline schema content resolved from the registry. Default behavior returns references without inline schema content.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "subject": "my-topic-value",
  • "id": 1,
  • "version": 1,
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "schema": "string",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ],
  • "metadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },
    • "properties": {
      • "owner": "data-platform-team",
      • "classification": "internal"
      },
    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "ruleSet": {
    • "migrationRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ]
    }
}

Delete a specific version of a subject

Deletes a specific version of a schema under a subject. By default this performs a soft-delete, marking the version as deleted but retaining it in storage. To permanently remove the version, set permanent=true. A version MUST be soft-deleted before it can be permanently deleted. The string latest and -1 are NOT allowed for permanent deletes; an explicit numeric version MUST be specified. If the schema version is referenced by other schemas, the delete operation fails with a 42206 error. The subject's mode MUST NOT be READONLY or READONLY_OVERRIDE for this operation to succeed.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the version from storage. The version MUST have been soft-deleted first.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
3

Get raw schema string by subject version

Retrieves only the raw schema string for the specified subject and version, without any metadata envelope. The version path parameter accepts an integer or latest. The optional format query parameter allows requesting an alternative serialization.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
format
string

An optional format hint for the returned schema string.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}/schema

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
"string"

Get schema IDs that reference this version

Returns a list of schema IDs that reference the schema registered under the given subject and version. This is useful for determining downstream dependencies before deleting a schema.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}/referencedby

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • 5,
  • 12
]

Get subject metadata

Returns the metadata from the latest schema version registered under the given subject. This endpoint provides quick access to the data contract metadata (tags, properties, sensitive fields) without retrieving the full schema.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/metadata

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "tags": {
    • "team": [
      • "platform",
      • "data-eng"
      ]
    },
  • "properties": {
    • "owner": "data-platform-team",
    • "classification": "internal"
    },
  • "sensitive": [
    • "ssn",
    • "email"
    ]
}

Look up schema under a subject

Checks whether the given schema has been registered under the specified subject. If found, returns the subject name, schema ID, version, and the full schema details. This is a lookup-only operation that does NOT register the schema. The deleted query parameter controls whether soft-deleted versions are considered during the lookup. When normalize=true, the schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, also searches among soft-deleted versions.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the provided schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The schema definition to search for.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json

      • "email"
      ]

    • "migrationRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",

          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"

        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,

    • "encodingRules": [

        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",

        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"

Delete a subject

Deletes all schema versions registered under the specified subject. By default this performs a soft-delete, marking all versions as deleted but retaining them in storage. To permanently remove the subject and all its versions, set permanent=true. A subject MUST be soft-deleted before it can be permanently deleted. If any schema version under this subject is referenced by schemas in other subjects, the delete operation fails with a 42206 error. The subject's mode MUST NOT be READONLY or READONLY_OVERRIDE for this operation to succeed.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the subject and all its versions from storage. The subject MUST have been soft-deleted first.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • 1,
  • 2,
  • 3
]

[Context-scoped] List subjects

Context-scoped version of /subjects. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes soft-deleted subjects alongside active ones.

deletedOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns only subjects that have been soft-deleted.

subjectPrefix
string

Filters the results to subjects whose name starts with the given prefix.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-topic-value",
  • "other-topic-key"
]

context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes soft-deleted versions alongside active ones.

deletedOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns only versions that have been soft-deleted.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions

[Context-scoped] Register a new schema under a subject

Context-scoped version of POST /subjects/{subject}/versions. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the schema is canonicalized before storage and fingerprinting.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The schema definition as a string. For Avro, this is a JSON string. For Protobuf, this is the .proto file content. For JSON Schema, this is a JSON Schema document as a string.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that this schema depends on.

id
integer <int64>

An explicit schema ID to assign. This is used in IMPORT mode for migrating schemas while preserving their original IDs.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions

context

string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

integer or string

Controls how schema references are returned. When set to RESOLVED, each reference in the response includes inline schema content resolved from the registry. Default behavior returns references without inline schema content.

        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {

          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ]
    }
}

[Context-scoped] Delete a specific version of a subject

Context-scoped version of DELETE /subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the version from storage.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
3

[Context-scoped] Get raw schema string by subject version

Context-scoped version of /subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}/schema. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
format
string

An optional format hint for the returned schema string.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}/schema

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
"string"

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}/referencedby

[Context-scoped] Get subject metadata

Context-scoped version of GET /subjects/{subject}/metadata. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/metadata

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "tags": {
    • "team": [
      • "platform",
      • "data-eng"
      ]
    },
  • "properties": {
    • "owner": "data-platform-team",
    • "classification": "internal"
    },
  • "sensitive": [
    • "ssn",
    • "email"
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] Look up schema under a subject

Context-scoped version of POST /subjects/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, also searches among soft-deleted versions.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the provided schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The schema definition to search for.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "id": 0,
  • "version": 0,
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "schema": "string",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ],
  • "metadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },
    • "properties": {
      • "owner": "data-platform-team",
      • "classification": "internal"
      },
    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "ruleSet": {
    • "migrationRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",

[Context-scoped] Delete a subject

Context-scoped version of DELETE /subjects/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the subject and all its versions.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • 1,
  • 2,
  • 3
]

Config

Operations for managing compatibility configuration at the global and per-subject level. The compatibility level determines what changes are permitted when registering a new schema version. Supported levels are NONE, BACKWARD, BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE, FORWARD, FORWARD_TRANSITIVE, FULL, and FULL_TRANSITIVE.

Get global compatibility configuration

Returns the global compatibility configuration for the registry. The defaultToGlobal query parameter is accepted for Confluent API compatibility but has no effect on this endpoint (it is meaningful only on per-subject config endpoints).

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

Accepted for compatibility. Has no effect on the global config endpoint.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/config

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json

  • "compatibilityLevel": "BACKWARD",

Set global compatibility configuration

Updates the global compatibility configuration for the registry. The compatibility field MUST be set to a valid compatibility level. If the request body contains an empty compatibility field, the current global configuration is returned without modification (Confluent compatibility behavior).

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema:
required
compatibility
string
Enum: "NONE" "BACKWARD" "BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FORWARD" "FORWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FULL" "FULL_TRANSITIVE"

The desired compatibility level. MUST be one of the supported levels.

normalize
boolean

Whether schemas SHOULD be normalized before storage and compatibility checks.

validateFields
boolean

Whether reserved field validation SHOULD be enforced.

alias
string

An alias that redirects operations on this subject to another subject.

compatibilityGroup
string

A group name used to partition compatibility checks.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

aliasForDeks
string

Alias for DEK subjects. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

compatibilityPolicy
string

Compatibility policy. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/config

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type

  • "compatibility": "NONE",
  • "normalize": true,
  • "validateFields": true,
  • "alias": "string",

  • "aliasForDeks": "string",
  • "compatibilityPolicy": "string"
}

Delete global compatibility configuration

Deletes the global compatibility configuration, resetting it to the server default (BACKWARD). Returns the compatibility level that was in effect before deletion.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/config

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500

Get subject-level compatibility configuration

Returns the compatibility configuration for the specified subject. If the subject does not have a subject-level configuration and defaultToGlobal is set to true, the global configuration is returned as a fallback. If defaultToGlobal is false (the default) and no subject-level configuration exists, a 40408 error is returned.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, falls back to the global compatibility configuration if the subject does not have its own configuration.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/config/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json

Set subject-level compatibility configuration

Updates the compatibility configuration for the specified subject. The compatibility field MUST be set to a valid compatibility level. If the request body contains an empty compatibility field, the current configuration for this subject is returned without modification.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Request Body schema:
required
compatibility
string
Enum: "NONE" "BACKWARD" "BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FORWARD" "FORWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FULL" "FULL_TRANSITIVE"

The desired compatibility level. MUST be one of the supported levels.

normalize
boolean

Whether schemas SHOULD be normalized before storage and compatibility checks.

validateFields
boolean

Whether reserved field validation SHOULD be enforced.

alias
string

An alias that redirects operations on this subject to another subject.

compatibilityGroup
string

A group name used to partition compatibility checks.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

aliasForDeks
string

Alias for DEK subjects. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

compatibilityPolicy
string

Compatibility policy. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/config/{subject}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "compatibility": "NONE",
  • "normalize": true,
  • "validateFields": true,
  • "alias": "string",
  • "compatibilityGroup": "string",

Delete subject-level compatibility configuration

Deletes the subject-level compatibility configuration for the specified subject, causing the subject to inherit the global configuration. Returns the compatibility level that was in effect before deletion.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/config/{subject}

  • 404
  • 500

[Context-scoped] Get global compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of /config. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

Accepted for compatibility. Has no effect on the global config endpoint.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config

[Context-scoped] Set global compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of PUT /config. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Request Body schema:
required
compatibility
string
Enum: "NONE" "BACKWARD" "BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FORWARD" "FORWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FULL" "FULL_TRANSITIVE"

The desired compatibility level. MUST be one of the supported levels.

normalize
boolean

Whether schemas SHOULD be normalized before storage and compatibility checks.

validateFields
boolean

Whether reserved field validation SHOULD be enforced.

alias
string

An alias that redirects operations on this subject to another subject.

compatibilityGroup
string

A group name used to partition compatibility checks.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

aliasForDeks
string

Alias for DEK subjects. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

compatibilityPolicy
string

Compatibility policy. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type

  • "validateFields": true,

[Context-scoped] Get subject-level compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of /config/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, falls back to the global compatibility configuration.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "compatibilityLevel": "BACKWARD",
  • "normalize": true,
  • "validateFields": true,
  • "alias": "string",

[Context-scoped] Set subject-level compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of PUT /config/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Request Body schema:
required
compatibility
string
Enum: "NONE" "BACKWARD" "BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FORWARD" "FORWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FULL" "FULL_TRANSITIVE"

The desired compatibility level. MUST be one of the supported levels.

normalize
boolean

Whether schemas SHOULD be normalized before storage and compatibility checks.

validateFields
boolean

Whether reserved field validation SHOULD be enforced.

alias
string

An alias that redirects operations on this subject to another subject.

compatibilityGroup
string

A group name used to partition compatibility checks.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

aliasForDeks
string

Alias for DEK subjects. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

compatibilityPolicy
string

Compatibility policy. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config/{subject}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "compatibility": "NONE",
  • "normalize": true,
  • "validateFields": true,
  • "alias": "string",
  • "compatibilityGroup": "string",
  • "defaultMetadata": {
    • "tags": {

[Context-scoped] Delete subject-level compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of DELETE /config/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config/{subject}

Mode

Operations for managing the registry mode at the global and per-subject level. The mode controls whether schema registration (writes) is permitted. Supported modes are READWRITE, READONLY, READONLY_OVERRIDE, and IMPORT.

Get global mode

Returns the global registry mode. The mode controls whether the registry accepts schema registration (write) operations. Possible modes are READWRITE (default), READONLY, READONLY_OVERRIDE, and IMPORT.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/mode

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Set global mode

Updates the global registry mode. The mode field MUST be set to a valid mode string (READWRITE, READONLY, READONLY_OVERRIDE, or IMPORT). The force query parameter MAY be set to true to bypass validation checks when changing mode.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
query Parameters
force
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, forces the mode change even if validation would otherwise prevent it.

Request Body schema:
required
mode
required
string
Enum: "READWRITE" "READONLY" "READONLY_OVERRIDE" "IMPORT"

The desired mode. MUST be one of the supported mode values.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/mode

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Delete global mode

Resets the global registry mode to the default (READWRITE) by removing any stored global mode override. Returns the previous mode that was in effect before the reset.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/mode

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Get subject-level mode

Returns the mode configured for the specified subject. If the subject does not have a subject-level mode and defaultToGlobal is set to true, the global mode is returned. If defaultToGlobal is false (the default) and no subject-level mode exists, a 40409 error is returned.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, falls back to the global mode if the subject does not have its own mode configured.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/mode/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Set subject-level mode

Updates the mode for the specified subject. The mode field MUST be a valid mode string. The force query parameter MAY be set to true to bypass validation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
force
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, forces the mode change even if validation would otherwise prevent it.

Request Body schema:
required
mode
required
string
Enum: "READWRITE" "READONLY" "READONLY_OVERRIDE" "IMPORT"

The desired mode. MUST be one of the supported mode values.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/mode/{subject}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Delete subject-level mode

Deletes the subject-level mode for the specified subject, causing it to inherit the global mode. Returns the mode that was in effect before deletion.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/mode/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

[Context-scoped] Get global mode

Context-scoped version of /mode. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

[Context-scoped] Set global mode

Context-scoped version of PUT /mode. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

query Parameters
force
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, forces the mode change.

Request Body schema:
required
mode
required
string
Enum: "READWRITE" "READONLY" "READONLY_OVERRIDE" "IMPORT"

The desired mode. MUST be one of the supported mode values.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

[Context-scoped] Delete global mode

Context-scoped version of DELETE /mode. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

[Context-scoped] Get subject-level mode

Context-scoped version of /mode/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, falls back to the global mode.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
force
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, forces the mode change.

Request Body schema:
required
mode
required
string
Enum: "READWRITE" "READONLY" "READONLY_OVERRIDE" "IMPORT"

The desired mode. MUST be one of the supported mode values.

Responses

path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject

string

Compatibility

Operations for testing whether a candidate schema is compatible with existing schema versions under a subject, without actually registering it.

Check compatibility against a specific version

Tests whether a candidate schema is compatible with the schema registered under the given subject at the specified version, according to the subject's compatibility policy. This is a read-only check that does NOT register the schema. The version path parameter accepts an integer or the string latest. When verbose=true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages explaining any incompatibilities found. When normalize=true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
verbose
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema definition to check for compatibility.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the candidate schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that the candidate schema depends on.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "is_compatible": true,
  • "messages": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

Check compatibility against all versions

Tests whether a candidate schema is compatible with all existing schema versions registered under the given subject, according to the subject's compatibility policy. This endpoint is equivalent to checking compatibility against latest when the compatibility level is non-transitive, or against all versions when the level is transitive. This is a read-only check that does NOT register the schema. When verbose=true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages. When normalize=true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
verbose
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema definition to check for compatibility.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the candidate schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that the candidate schema depends on.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "is_compatible": true,
  • "messages": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] Check compatibility against a specific version

Context-scoped version of POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
verbose
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema definition to check for compatibility.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the candidate schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that the candidate schema depends on.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "is_compatible": true,
  • "messages": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] Check compatibility against all versions

Context-scoped version of POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
verbose
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema definition to check for compatibility.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the candidate schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that the candidate schema depends on.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404

Content type

  • "messages": [
    • "string"
