Import

Operations for bulk-importing schemas from another schema registry, preserving original schema IDs. This is used for migration scenarios.

Bulk import schemas

Imports multiple schemas in a single request, preserving the original schema IDs from the source registry. This endpoint is intended for migrating schemas from another schema registry (e.g. Confluent Schema Registry). Each schema in the request MUST include a specific id, subject, version, and schema string. The response indicates how many schemas were successfully imported and how many failed, along with individual results for each schema in the request.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema:
required
required
Array of objects (ImportSchemaRequest)

The list of schemas to import. Each entry MUST include an explicit ID, subject, version, and schema content.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/import/schemas

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schemas": [
    • {
      • "id": 0,
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "schemaType": "AVRO",
      • "schema": "string",
      • "references": [
        • {
          • "name": "com.example.Address",
          • "subject": "address-value",
          • "version": 1
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "imported": 10,
  • "errors": 2,
  • "results": [
    • {
      • "id": 0,
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "success": true,
      • "error": "string"
      }
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] Bulk import schemas

Context-scoped version of POST /import/schemas. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Request Body schema:
required
required
Array of objects (ImportSchemaRequest)

The list of schemas to import. Each entry MUST include an explicit ID, subject, version, and schema content.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/import/schemas

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schemas": [
    • {
      • "id": 0,
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "schemaType": "AVRO",
      • "schema": "string",
      • "references": [
        • {
          • "name": "com.example.Address",
          • "subject": "address-value",
          • "version": 1
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "imported": 10,
  • "errors": 2,
  • "results": [
    • {
      • "id": 0,
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "success": true,
      • "error": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Exporters

Operations for managing schema exporters. Exporters enable Schema Linking by replicating schemas from one registry to another. Each exporter has a name, a context, an optional subject filter, and configuration for connecting to the destination registry. Exporters can be paused, resumed, and reset. This API follows the Confluent Schema Linking format.

List exporters

Returns a list of all exporter names registered in the registry. The response is an array of strings, each representing the name of an exporter.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-exporter",
  • "backup-exporter"
]

Create an exporter

Creates a new schema exporter with the specified configuration. The exporter name MUST be unique within the registry. The contextType field specifies whether the exporter operates on a custom context or the default context. The subjects array MAY be used to filter which subjects are exported. The subjectRenameFormat allows renaming subjects during export using a template string.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema:
required
name
required
string

The unique name for the exporter. MUST be non-empty and unique within the registry. Ignored on update operations (the name in the URL path is used).

contextType
string
Default: "AUTO"
Enum: "CUSTOM" "AUTO" "NONE"

The type of context for the exporter. CUSTOM indicates a user-defined context, AUTO indicates the default context, and NONE indicates no context scoping.

context
string

The context name to use when contextType is CUSTOM. Ignored when contextType is AUTO or NONE.

subjects
Array of strings

An optional list of subject names to export. If empty or omitted, all subjects are exported.

subjectRenameFormat
string

An optional rename format template applied to subject names during export. Use ${subject} as a placeholder for the original subject name.

object

A map of configuration key-value pairs for the exporter. These typically include the destination schema registry URL and authentication credentials.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "name": "my-exporter",
  • "contextType": "AUTO",
  • "context": ".my-context",
  • "subjects": [
    • "my-topic-value",
    • "my-topic-key"
    ],
  • "subjectRenameFormat": "dest-${subject}",
  • "config": {
    • "schema.registry.url": "http://destination:8081",
    • "basic.auth.credentials.source": "USER_INFO",
    • "basic.auth.user.info": "user:password"
    }
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 409
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

Get exporter info

Returns the full configuration and details of the specified exporter, including its name, context type, context, subject filter, subject rename format, and configuration map.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter",
  • "contextType": "AUTO",
  • "context": ".my-context",
  • "subjects": [
    • "my-topic-value"
    ],
  • "subjectRenameFormat": "dest-${subject}",
  • "config": {
    • "schema.registry.url": "http://destination:8081"
    }
}

Update an exporter

Updates the configuration of the specified exporter. All fields in the request body are applied as the new configuration. The exporter name cannot be changed.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Request Body schema:
required
name
required
string

The unique name for the exporter. MUST be non-empty and unique within the registry. Ignored on update operations (the name in the URL path is used).

contextType
string
Default: "AUTO"
Enum: "CUSTOM" "AUTO" "NONE"

The type of context for the exporter. CUSTOM indicates a user-defined context, AUTO indicates the default context, and NONE indicates no context scoping.

context
string

The context name to use when contextType is CUSTOM. Ignored when contextType is AUTO or NONE.

subjects
Array of strings

An optional list of subject names to export. If empty or omitted, all subjects are exported.

subjectRenameFormat
string

An optional rename format template applied to subject names during export. Use ${subject} as a placeholder for the original subject name.

object

A map of configuration key-value pairs for the exporter. These typically include the destination schema registry URL and authentication credentials.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "name": "my-exporter",
  • "contextType": "AUTO",
  • "context": ".my-context",
  • "subjects": [
    • "my-topic-value",
    • "my-topic-key"
    ],
  • "subjectRenameFormat": "dest-${subject}",
  • "config": {
    • "schema.registry.url": "http://destination:8081",
    • "basic.auth.credentials.source": "USER_INFO",
    • "basic.auth.user.info": "user:password"
    }
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

Delete an exporter

Deletes the specified exporter. The exporter MUST be in a paused or starting state before it can be deleted. Returns the name of the deleted exporter.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

Pause an exporter

Pauses the specified exporter. A paused exporter stops replicating schemas to the destination registry. The exporter can be resumed later with the resume endpoint.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}/pause

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

Resume an exporter

Resumes a previously paused exporter. The exporter will continue replicating schemas from where it left off.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}/resume

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

Reset an exporter

Resets the offset of the specified exporter. The exporter MUST be in a paused state before it can be reset. After resetting, the exporter will re-export all matching schemas from the beginning when resumed.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}/reset

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

Get exporter status

Returns the current status of the specified exporter, including its state (STARTING, RUNNING, PAUSED, or ERROR), the current offset, the last updated timestamp, and any error trace if the exporter is in an error state.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}/status

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter",
  • "state": "RUNNING",
  • "offset": 42,
  • "ts": 1706000000000,
  • "trace": ""
}

Get exporter config

Returns the configuration map of the specified exporter. The configuration map contains key-value pairs that define the exporter's connection and behavior settings for the destination registry.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}/config

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "schema.registry.url": "http://destination:8081",
  • "basic.auth.credentials.source": "USER_INFO",
  • "basic.auth.user.info": "user:password"
}

Update exporter config

Updates the configuration map of the specified exporter. The request body MUST be a JSON object of key-value string pairs representing the new configuration. This replaces the entire configuration map.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Request Body schema:
required
property name*
additional property
string

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/exporters/{name}/config

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "property1": "string",
  • "property2": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

[Context-scoped] List exporters

Context-scoped version of GET /exporters. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/exporters

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-exporter",
  • "backup-exporter"
]

[Context-scoped] Create an exporter

Context-scoped version of POST /exporters. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Request Body schema:
required
name
required
string

The unique name for the exporter. MUST be non-empty and unique within the registry. Ignored on update operations (the name in the URL path is used).

contextType
string
Default: "AUTO"
Enum: "CUSTOM" "AUTO" "NONE"

The type of context for the exporter. CUSTOM indicates a user-defined context, AUTO indicates the default context, and NONE indicates no context scoping.

context
string

The context name to use when contextType is CUSTOM. Ignored when contextType is AUTO or NONE.

subjects
Array of strings

An optional list of subject names to export. If empty or omitted, all subjects are exported.

subjectRenameFormat
string

An optional rename format template applied to subject names during export. Use ${subject} as a placeholder for the original subject name.

object

A map of configuration key-value pairs for the exporter. These typically include the destination schema registry URL and authentication credentials.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/exporters

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "name": "my-exporter",
  • "contextType": "AUTO",
  • "context": ".my-context",
  • "subjects": [
    • "my-topic-value",
    • "my-topic-key"
    ],
  • "subjectRenameFormat": "dest-${subject}",
  • "config": {
    • "schema.registry.url": "http://destination:8081",
    • "basic.auth.credentials.source": "USER_INFO",
    • "basic.auth.user.info": "user:password"
    }
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 409
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

required

string

contextType
string
Default: "AUTO"

The type of context for the exporter. CUSTOM indicates a user-defined context, AUTO indicates the default context, and NONE indicates no context scoping.

context
string

The context name to use when contextType is CUSTOM. Ignored when contextType is AUTO or NONE.

subjects
Array of strings

An optional list of subject names to export. If empty or omitted, all subjects are exported.

subjectRenameFormat
string

An optional rename format template applied to subject names during export. Use ${subject} as a placeholder for the original subject name.

object

A map of configuration key-value pairs for the exporter. These typically include the destination schema registry URL and authentication credentials.

  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

Contexts

Operations for managing schema registry contexts. Contexts provide multi-tenant schema isolation — each context has its own independent schema IDs, subjects, versions, compatibility config, and modes. Subjects are qualified with a context prefix using the Confluent-compatible format :.contextname:subject. All standard registry routes are also available under /contexts/{context}/... for context-scoped access.

Get schema registry contexts

Returns the list of contexts defined in the registry. Contexts provide multi-tenant schema isolation — each context has its own independent schema IDs, subjects, versions, compatibility config, and modes.

The default context "." is always present, even when no schemas have been registered. Additional contexts are created implicitly when a schema is registered with a context-qualified subject name (e.g. :.team-a:my-subject).

Context-qualified subject format: :.contextname:subject (Confluent-compatible). All standard registry endpoints accept qualified subjects. Alternatively, all registry routes are available under the /contexts/{context}/... URL prefix for context-scoped access.

Context names MUST match ^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$ and MUST NOT exceed 255 characters. Names are normalized with a leading dot (e.g. team-a becomes .team-a). Context names are case-sensitive.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • ".",
  • ".team-a",
  • ".team-b"
]

path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$

Responses

Response samples

  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json

  • "PROTOBUF",
  • "JSON"
]

[Context-scoped] List schemas

Context-scoped version of /schemas. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

query Parameters
subjectPrefix
string

Filter results to schemas whose subject name starts with this prefix.

deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, soft-deleted schemas are included in the results.

latestOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, only the latest version of each subject is returned.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return. If omitted or set to 0, all results are returned.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/schemas

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • {
    • "subject": "string",
    • "version": 0,
    • "id": 0,
    • "schemaType": "AVRO",
    • "schema": "string",
    • "references": [
      • {
        • "name": "com.example.Address",
        • "subject": "address-value",
        • "version": 1
        }
      ],
    • "metadata": {
      • "tags": {
        • "team": [
          • "platform",
          • "data-eng"
          ]
        },
      • "properties": {
        • "owner": "data-platform-team",
        • "classification": "internal"
        },
      • "sensitive": [
        • "ssn",
        • "email"
        ]
      },
    • "ruleSet": {
      • "migrationRules": [
        • {
          • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
          • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
          • "kind": "CONDITION",
          • "mode": "WRITE",
          • "type": "CEL",
          • "tags": [
            • "string"
            ],
          • "params": {
            • "property1": "string",
            • "property2": "string"
            },
          • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
          • "onSuccess": "string",
          • "onFailure": "string",
          • "disabled": false,
          • "enableAt": 0
          }
        ],
      • "domainRules": [
        • {
          • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
          • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
          • "kind": "CONDITION",
          • "mode": "WRITE",
          • "type": "CEL",
          • "tags": [
            • "string"
            ],
          • "params": {
            • "property1": "string",
            • "property2": "string"
            },
          • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
          • "onSuccess": "string",
          • "onFailure": "string",
          • "disabled": false,
          • "enableAt": 0
          }
        ],
      • "encodingRules": [
        • {
          • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
          • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
          • "kind": "CONDITION",
          • "mode": "WRITE",
          • "type": "CEL",
          • "tags": [
            • "string"
            ],
          • "params": {
            • "property1": "string",
            • "property2": "string"
            },
          • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
          • "onSuccess": "string",
          • "onFailure": "string",
          • "disabled": false,
          • "enableAt": 0
          }
        ]
      }
    }
]

[Context-scoped] Get schema by global ID

Context-scoped version of /schemas/ids/{id}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The globally unique integer ID of the schema.

query Parameters
format
string

An optional format hint for the returned schema string.

fetchMaxId
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes a maxId field containing the current highest schema ID in the registry.

subject
string

An optional subject name filter.

referenceFormat
string
Value: "RESOLVED"

Controls how schema references are returned. When set to RESOLVED, each reference in the response includes inline schema content resolved from the registry. Default behavior returns references without inline schema content.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/schemas/ids/{id}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ],
  • "metadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },
    • "properties": {
      • "owner": "data-platform-team",
      • "classification": "internal"
      },
    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "ruleSet": {
    • "migrationRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }

  • "maxId": 0
}

required

[Context-scoped] List subjects

Context-scoped version of /subjects. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes soft-deleted subjects alongside active ones.

deletedOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns only subjects that have been soft-deleted.

subjectPrefix
string

Filters the results to subjects whose name starts with the given prefix.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-topic-value",
  • "other-topic-key"
]

[Context-scoped] List versions under a subject

Context-scoped version of /subjects/{subject}/versions. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, includes soft-deleted versions alongside active ones.

deletedOnly
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns only versions that have been soft-deleted.

offset
integer >= 0
Default: 0

The number of results to skip for pagination.

limit
integer >= 0

The maximum number of results to return.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • 1,
  • 2,
  • 3
]

[Context-scoped] Register a new schema under a subject

Context-scoped version of POST /subjects/{subject}/versions. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the schema is canonicalized before storage and fingerprinting.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The schema definition as a string. For Avro, this is a JSON string. For Protobuf, this is the .proto file content. For JSON Schema, this is a JSON Schema document as a string.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that this schema depends on.

id
integer <int64>

An explicit schema ID to assign. This is used in IMPORT mode for migrating schemas while preserving their original IDs.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "{\"type\":\"record\",\"name\":\"User\",\"fields\":[{\"name\":\"name\",\"type\":\"string\"}]}",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ],
  • "id": 0,
  • "metadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },
    • "properties": {
      • "owner": "data-platform-team",
      • "classification": "internal"
      },
    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "ruleSet": {
    • "migrationRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ]
    }
}

[Context-scoped] Get a specific version of a subject

Context-scoped version of /subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, soft-deleted versions are also retrievable.

format
string

An optional format hint for the returned schema string.

referenceFormat
string
Value: "RESOLVED"

Controls how schema references are returned. When set to RESOLVED, each reference in the response includes inline schema content resolved from the registry. Default behavior returns references without inline schema content.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "subject": "my-topic-value",
  • "id": 1,
  • "version": 1,
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "schema": "string",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ],
  • "metadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },

    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "ruleSet": {

      • {

        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",

          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },
        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",
        • "disabled": false,
        • "enableAt": 0
        }
      ],
    • "domainRules": [
      • {

        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",

          • "string"

        • "params": {

          },

        • "onSuccess": "string",

        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {

[Context-scoped] Delete a specific version of a subject

Context-scoped version of DELETE /subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the version from storage.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
3

required

[Context-scoped] Look up schema under a subject

Context-scoped version of POST /subjects/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, also searches among soft-deleted versions.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the provided schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The schema definition to search for.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}

Request samples

Content type

  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] Delete a subject

Context-scoped version of DELETE /subjects/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the subject and all its versions.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/subjects/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json

  • 3

[Context-scoped] Get global compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of /config. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

Accepted for compatibility. Has no effect on the global config endpoint.

Responses

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config

[Context-scoped] Set global compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of PUT /config. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Request Body schema:
required
compatibility
string
Enum: "NONE" "BACKWARD" "BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FORWARD" "FORWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FULL" "FULL_TRANSITIVE"

The desired compatibility level. MUST be one of the supported levels.

normalize
boolean

Whether schemas SHOULD be normalized before storage and compatibility checks.

validateFields
boolean

Whether reserved field validation SHOULD be enforced.

alias
string

An alias that redirects operations on this subject to another subject.

compatibilityGroup
string

A group name used to partition compatibility checks.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

aliasForDeks
string

Alias for DEK subjects. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

compatibilityPolicy
string

Compatibility policy. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "compatibility": "NONE",
  • "normalize": true,
  • "validateFields": true,
  • "alias": "string",
  • "compatibilityGroup": "string",
  • "defaultMetadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",

      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",
        • "tags": [
          • "string"
          ],
        • "params": {
          • "property1": "string",
          • "property2": "string"
          },

        • "onSuccess": "string",
        • "onFailure": "string",

        }
      ],
    • "encodingRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",

        • "type": "CEL",

[Context-scoped] Delete global compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of DELETE /config. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config

[Context-scoped] Get subject-level compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of /config/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, falls back to the global compatibility configuration.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config/{subject}

[Context-scoped] Set subject-level compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of PUT /config/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Request Body schema:
required
compatibility
string
Enum: "NONE" "BACKWARD" "BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FORWARD" "FORWARD_TRANSITIVE" "FULL" "FULL_TRANSITIVE"

The desired compatibility level. MUST be one of the supported levels.

normalize
boolean

Whether schemas SHOULD be normalized before storage and compatibility checks.

validateFields
boolean

Whether reserved field validation SHOULD be enforced.

alias
string

An alias that redirects operations on this subject to another subject.

compatibilityGroup
string

A group name used to partition compatibility checks.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (Metadata)

Metadata associated with a schema for data contract management. Contains tags for categorization, properties for key-value data, and a list of field names that contain sensitive information.

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

object (RuleSet)

A set of data contract rules attached to a schema. Contains migration rules (applied during schema evolution), domain rules (applied during data processing), and encoding rules (applied during serialization/deserialization).

aliasForDeks
string

Alias for DEK subjects. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

compatibilityPolicy
string

Compatibility policy. Enterprise pass-through field — accepted, stored, and returned but not enforced.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config/{subject}

Content type

        • "expr": "message.ssn != ''",
        • "onSuccess": "string",

        }

[Context-scoped] Delete subject-level compatibility configuration

Context-scoped version of DELETE /config/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/config/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "compatibilityLevel": "BACKWARD",
  • "normalize": true,
  • "validateFields": true,
  • "alias": "string",
  • "compatibilityGroup": "string",
  • "defaultMetadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },
    • "properties": {
      • "owner": "data-platform-team",
      • "classification": "internal"
      },
    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "overrideMetadata": {
    • "tags": {
      • "team": [
        • "platform",
        • "data-eng"
        ]
      },
    • "properties": {
      • "owner": "data-platform-team",
      • "classification": "internal"
      },
    • "sensitive": [
      • "ssn",
      • "email"
      ]
    },
  • "defaultRuleSet": {
    • "migrationRules": [
      • {
        • "name": "checkSensitiveFields",
        • "doc": "Ensures PII fields are encrypted",
        • "kind": "CONDITION",
        • "mode": "WRITE",
        • "type": "CEL",

          ],

[Context-scoped] Get global mode

Context-scoped version of /mode. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

[Context-scoped] Set global mode

Context-scoped version of PUT /mode. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

query Parameters
force
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, forces the mode change.

Request Body schema:
required
mode
required
string
Enum: "READWRITE" "READONLY" "READONLY_OVERRIDE" "IMPORT"

The desired mode. MUST be one of the supported mode values.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

[Context-scoped] Delete global mode

Context-scoped version of DELETE /mode. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

[Context-scoped] Get subject-level mode

Context-scoped version of /mode/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
defaultToGlobal
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, falls back to the global mode.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode/{subject}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Context-scoped version of PUT /mode/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
force
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, forces the mode change.

Request Body schema:
required
mode
required
string
Enum: "READWRITE" "READONLY" "READONLY_OVERRIDE" "IMPORT"

The desired mode. MUST be one of the supported mode values.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/mode/{subject}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "mode": "READWRITE"
}

Context-scoped version of DELETE /mode/{subject}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

Responses

Local development server

[Context-scoped] Check compatibility against a specific version

Context-scoped version of POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

required
integer or string

The version number to operate on. MUST be a positive integer (1 through 2^31-1) or the string latest to refer to the most recently registered version. The value -1 is also accepted as an alias for latest.

query Parameters
verbose
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema definition to check for compatibility.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the candidate schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that the candidate schema depends on.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "is_compatible": true,
  • "messages": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] Check compatibility against all versions

Context-scoped version of POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

subject
required
string

The name of the subject. Subjects typically correspond to Kafka topic names with a -key or -value suffix (e.g. my-topic-value).

query Parameters
verbose
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the response includes detailed compatibility messages.

normalize
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, the candidate schema is canonicalized before comparison.

Request Body schema:
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema definition to check for compatibility.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The type of the candidate schema. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Array of objects (Reference)

References to other schemas that the candidate schema depends on.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "references": [
    • {
      • "name": "com.example.Address",
      • "subject": "address-value",
      • "version": 1
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "is_compatible": true,
  • "messages": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] Bulk import schemas

Context-scoped version of POST /import/schemas. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Request Body schema:
required
required
Array of objects (ImportSchemaRequest)

The list of schemas to import. Each entry MUST include an explicit ID, subject, version, and schema content.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/import/schemas

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "schemas": [
    • {
      • "id": 0,
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "schemaType": "AVRO",
      • "schema": "string",
      • "references": [
        • {
          • "name": "com.example.Address",
          • "subject": "address-value",
          • "version": 1
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "imported": 10,
  • "errors": 2,
  • "results": [
    • {
      • "id": 0,
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "success": true,
      • "error": "string"
      }
    ]
}

[Context-scoped] List exporters

Context-scoped version of GET /exporters. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/exporters

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-exporter",
  • "backup-exporter"
]

[Context-scoped] Create an exporter

Context-scoped version of POST /exporters. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Request Body schema:
required
name
required
string

The unique name for the exporter. MUST be non-empty and unique within the registry. Ignored on update operations (the name in the URL path is used).

contextType
string
Default: "AUTO"
Enum: "CUSTOM" "AUTO" "NONE"

The type of context for the exporter. CUSTOM indicates a user-defined context, AUTO indicates the default context, and NONE indicates no context scoping.

context
string

The context name to use when contextType is CUSTOM. Ignored when contextType is AUTO or NONE.

subjects
Array of strings

An optional list of subject names to export. If empty or omitted, all subjects are exported.

subjectRenameFormat
string

An optional rename format template applied to subject names during export. Use ${subject} as a placeholder for the original subject name.

object

A map of configuration key-value pairs for the exporter. These typically include the destination schema registry URL and authentication credentials.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/exporters

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "name": "my-exporter",
  • "contextType": "AUTO",
  • "context": ".my-context",
  • "subjects": [
    • "my-topic-value",
    • "my-topic-key"
    ],
  • "subjectRenameFormat": "dest-${subject}",
  • "config": {
    • "schema.registry.url": "http://destination:8081",
    • "basic.auth.credentials.source": "USER_INFO",
    • "basic.auth.user.info": "user:password"
    }
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 409
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}

    • "my-topic-value"

context
required

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Request Body schema:
required
name
required
string

The unique name for the exporter. MUST be non-empty and unique within the registry. Ignored on update operations (the name in the URL path is used).

contextType
string
Default: "AUTO"
Enum: "CUSTOM" "AUTO" "NONE"

The type of context for the exporter. CUSTOM indicates a user-defined context, AUTO indicates the default context, and NONE indicates no context scoping.

context
string

The context name to use when contextType is CUSTOM. Ignored when contextType is AUTO or NONE.

subjects
Array of strings

An optional list of subject names to export. If empty or omitted, all subjects are exported.

subjectRenameFormat
string

An optional rename format template applied to subject names during export. Use ${subject} as a placeholder for the original subject name.

object

A map of configuration key-value pairs for the exporter. These typically include the destination schema registry URL and authentication credentials.

Responses

    • "my-topic-value",
    • "my-topic-key"
    ],
  • "subjectRenameFormat": "dest-${subject}",
  • "config": {
    • "schema.registry.url": "http://destination:8081",
    • "basic.auth.credentials.source": "USER_INFO",
    • "basic.auth.user.info": "user:password"
    }
}

  • 404

application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json

  • "name": "my-exporter"

[Context-scoped] Delete an exporter

Authorizations:

path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$

}

[Context-scoped] Pause an exporter

Context-scoped version of PUT /exporters/{name}/pause. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

name
required
string

The name of the exporter.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/exporters/{name}/pause

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-exporter"
}
