Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/statistics

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{ }

[Context-scoped] Check field consistency

Context-scoped version of /statistics/fields/{field}. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

field
required
string

The field name.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/statistics/fields/{field}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{ }

[Context-scoped] Detect schema patterns

Context-scoped version of /statistics/patterns. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/statistics/patterns

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{ }

Metadata

Operations for retrieving registry metadata such as the cluster ID and server version.

[Context-scoped] Get cluster ID

Context-scoped version of /v1/metadata/id. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/v1/metadata/id

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "id": "default-cluster"
}

[Context-scoped] Get server version

Context-scoped version of /v1/metadata/version. See the root-level operation for full documentation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/v1/metadata/version

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "version": "1.0.0",
  • "commit": "abc123def",
  • "build_time": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Get cluster ID

Returns the cluster ID of this schema registry instance. The cluster ID is a string identifier configured at server startup.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/v1/metadata/id

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "id": "default-cluster"
}

Get server version

Returns the version, commit hash, and build time of the running schema registry server.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/v1/metadata/version

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "version": "1.0.0",
  • "commit": "abc123def",
  • "build_time": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Account

Self-service account endpoints for authenticated users to view their own profile and change their password.

Get current user

Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user. The caller MUST be authenticated. If the user record is not found in the database, a 404 error is returned.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/me

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "email": "johndoe@example.com",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Change current user password

Changes the password of the currently authenticated user. The request MUST include both the current (old) password for verification and the desired new password. Returns 204 No Content on success.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
old_password
required
string <password>

The current password for verification.

new_password
required
string <password>

The desired new password.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/me/password

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "old_password": "pa$$word",
  • "new_password": "pa$$word"
}

Response samples

  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "error_code": 40401,
  • "message": "Subject 'my-topic-value' not found"
}

Admin

Administrative endpoints for managing users, API keys, and roles. These endpoints require admin-level permissions.

List all users

Returns a list of all users in the registry. The caller MUST have admin read permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/users

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "users": [
    • {
      • "id": 1,
      • "username": "johndoe",
      • "email": "johndoe@example.com",
      • "role": "developer",
      • "enabled": true,
      • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      • "updated_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
      }
    ]
}

Create a new user

Creates a new user in the registry. The username, password, and role fields are required. The role MUST be one of super_admin, admin, developer, or readonly. The caller MUST have admin write permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
username
required
string

The unique username for the new user.

email
string

The email address for the user.

password
required
string <password>

The password for the new user.

role
required
string
Enum: "super_admin" "admin" "developer" "readonly"

The role to assign to the user. Determines the user's permissions.

enabled
boolean
Default: true

Whether the user account is enabled. Defaults to true if omitted.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/users

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "email": "johndoe@example.com",
  • "password": "SecureP@ss123",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "enabled": true
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 409
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "email": "johndoe@example.com",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Get a user by ID

Retrieves the user with the specified ID. The caller MUST have admin read permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/users/{id}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "email": "johndoe@example.com",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Update a user

Updates one or more fields of the user with the specified ID. Only the fields provided in the request body are modified; omitted fields remain unchanged. The caller MUST have admin write permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
email
string

The new email address.

password
string <password>

The new password.

role
string
Enum: "super_admin" "admin" "developer" "readonly"

The new role.

enabled
boolean

Whether the user account is enabled.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/users/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "email": "string",
  • "password": "pa$$word",
  • "role": "super_admin",
  • "enabled": true
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "email": "johndoe@example.com",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "updated_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}

Delete a user

Permanently deletes the user with the specified ID. Returns 204 No Content on success. The caller MUST have admin write permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/users/{id}

Response samples

  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "error_code": 40401,
  • "message": "Subject 'my-topic-value' not found"
}

List API keys

Returns a list of all API keys in the registry. The optional user_id query parameter filters results to API keys owned by a specific user. The caller MUST have admin read permissions. The raw key secret is never included in list responses.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
query Parameters
user_id
integer <int64>

Filter API keys by the owning user's ID. If omitted, all API keys are returned.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/apikeys

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "api_keys": [
    • {
      • "id": 1,
      • "key_prefix": "axon_abc",
      • "name": "ci-pipeline-key",
      • "role": "developer",
      • "user_id": 1,
      • "username": "johndoe",
      • "enabled": true,
      • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      • "expires_at": "2025-02-14T10:30:00Z",
      • "last_used": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
      }
    ]
}

Create a new API key

Creates a new API key. The name, role, and expires_in fields are required. The name MUST be unique per user. The expires_in value is a duration in seconds from the current time (e.g. 2592000 for 30 days). The role MUST be one of super_admin, admin, developer, or readonly. The raw API key secret is returned ONLY in the creation response and cannot be retrieved afterward. Clients SHOULD store the key securely immediately after creation. Super admins MAY create API keys for other users by specifying the for_user_id field.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
name
required
string

A human-readable name for the API key. MUST be unique per user.

role
required
string
Enum: "super_admin" "admin" "developer" "readonly"

The role to assign to the API key. Determines what operations the key can perform.

expires_in
required
integer <int64>

The duration in seconds until the API key expires. For example, 2592000 for 30 days. MUST be a positive integer.

for_user_id
integer <int64>

The ID of the user who SHOULD own this API key. Only super admins MAY create API keys for other users. If omitted, the key is created for the authenticated user.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/apikeys

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "name": "ci-pipeline-key",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "expires_in": 2592000,
  • "for_user_id": 0
}

Response samples

  • 201
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 409
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 0,
  • "key": "axon_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890",
  • "key_prefix": "string",
  • "name": "string",
  • "role": "string",
  • "user_id": 0,
  • "username": "string",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  • "expires_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}

Get an API key by ID

Retrieves the API key with the specified ID. The raw key secret is NOT included in the response. The caller MUST have admin read permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/apikeys/{id}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "key_prefix": "axon_abc",
  • "name": "ci-pipeline-key",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "user_id": 1,
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "expires_at": "2025-02-14T10:30:00Z",
  • "last_used": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}

Update an API key

Updates one or more fields of the API key with the specified ID. Only the fields provided in the request body are modified; omitted fields remain unchanged. The caller MUST have admin write permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
name
string

The new name for the API key.

role
string
Enum: "super_admin" "admin" "developer" "readonly"

The new role for the API key.

enabled
boolean

Whether the API key is enabled.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/apikeys/{id}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "name": "string",
  • "role": "super_admin",
  • "enabled": true
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "key_prefix": "axon_abc",
  • "name": "ci-pipeline-key",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "user_id": 1,
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "expires_at": "2025-02-14T10:30:00Z",
  • "last_used": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}

Delete an API key

Permanently deletes the API key with the specified ID. Returns 204 No Content on success. The caller MUST have admin write permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/apikeys/{id}

Response samples

  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "error_code": 40401,
  • "message": "Subject 'my-topic-value' not found"
}

Revoke an API key

Revokes the API key with the specified ID by disabling it. The API key remains in the database but can no longer be used for authentication. The caller MUST have admin write permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/apikeys/{id}/revoke

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "id": 1,
  • "key_prefix": "axon_abc",
  • "name": "ci-pipeline-key",
  • "role": "developer",
  • "user_id": 1,
  • "username": "johndoe",
  • "enabled": true,
  • "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  • "expires_at": "2025-02-14T10:30:00Z",
  • "last_used": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}

Rotate an API key

Rotates the API key with the specified ID by revoking the existing key and creating a new one with the same name and role but a fresh secret and expiry. The expires_in field in the request body specifies the duration in seconds for the new key. The raw secret for the new key is returned ONLY in this response. The caller MUST have admin write permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
id
required
integer <int64> >= 1

The unique integer ID of the resource.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
expires_in
required
integer <int64>

The duration in seconds until the new API key expires. MUST be a positive integer.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/apikeys/{id}/rotate

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "expires_in": 2592000
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 401
  • 403
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "new_key": {
    • "id": 0,
    • "key": "axon_abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz1234567890",
    • "key_prefix": "string",
    • "name": "string",
    • "role": "string",
    • "user_id": 0,
    • "username": "string",
    • "enabled": true,
    • "created_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
    • "expires_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
    },
  • "revoked_id": 1
}

List available roles

Returns the list of roles available in the registry along with their descriptions and associated permissions. The caller MUST have admin read permissions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/admin/roles

Response samples

  • 200
  • 401
  • 403
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "roles": [
    • {
      • "name": "developer",
      • "description": "Can register and read schemas",
      • "permissions": [
        • "schema:read",
        • "schema:write",
        • "subject:read"
        ]
      }
    ]
}

Health

Kubernetes-style health check endpoints for liveness, readiness, and startup probes. These endpoints are unauthenticated and SHOULD be used for Kubernetes pod lifecycle management. The liveness probe confirms the process is alive, the readiness probe confirms the service can handle traffic (storage backend is reachable), and the startup probe confirms initialization is complete.

Health check (legacy)

Returns an empty JSON object to indicate the service is healthy and accepting requests. This endpoint does not require authentication. It is retained for backward compatibility with the Confluent Schema Registry API. For Kubernetes deployments, prefer the dedicated /health/live, /health/ready, and /health/startup endpoints.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{ }

Liveness check

Returns HTTP 200 unconditionally to confirm the process is alive and not deadlocked. This endpoint SHOULD be used as the Kubernetes livenessProbe target. It does not check storage backend connectivity — a liveness probe that depends on external services can cause unnecessary pod restarts during transient database outages.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/health/live

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "status": "UP"
}

Readiness check

Returns HTTP 200 when the service is ready to handle traffic, or HTTP 503 when the storage backend is unreachable. This endpoint SHOULD be used as the Kubernetes readinessProbe target. When the probe fails, Kubernetes removes the pod from Service endpoints so that traffic is routed only to healthy instances.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/health/ready

Response samples

  • 200
  • 503
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "status": "UP"
}

Startup check

Returns HTTP 200 when initialization is complete (storage backend connected and migrations applied), or HTTP 503 while the service is still starting up. This endpoint SHOULD be used as the Kubernetes startupProbe target. The startup probe prevents liveness and readiness probes from running until the service has finished initializing, avoiding premature pod restarts during slow Cassandra migrations or initial database connections.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/health/startup

Response samples

  • 200
  • 503
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "status": "UP"
}

DEK Registry

Operations for managing Data Encryption Keys (DEKs) and Key Encryption Keys (KEKs). KEKs are top-level encryption keys identified by name, associated with a KMS provider. DEKs are per-subject encryption keys managed under a KEK. The DEK Registry API follows the Confluent Schema Registry DEK Registry format and uses the /dek-registry/v1 prefix.

List KEK names

Returns a list of all Key Encryption Key (KEK) names registered in the DEK registry. The response is an array of strings, each representing the name of a KEK. Soft-deleted KEKs are excluded unless the deleted query parameter is set to true.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, soft-deleted KEKs are included in the results.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-kek",
  • "backup-kek"
]

Create a KEK

Creates a new Key Encryption Key (KEK) with the specified configuration. The KEK name MUST be unique within the registry. The kmsType specifies the Key Management Service provider (e.g. aws-kms, azure-kms, gcp-kms, hcvault, openbao). The kmsKeyId identifies the master key in the KMS. The shared flag indicates whether this KEK is shared across multiple schema subjects.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema:
required
name
required
string

The unique name of the KEK. MUST be unique within the registry.

kmsType
required
string

The Key Management Service provider type (e.g. aws-kms, azure-kms, gcp-kms, hcvault, openbao).

kmsKeyId
required
string

The identifier of the master key in the KMS. The format depends on the KMS provider.

object

Additional properties for the KMS provider. The keys and values depend on the KMS type.

doc
string

A human-readable description or documentation string for this KEK.

shared
boolean
Default: false

Whether this KEK is shared across multiple schema subjects. When true, the same KEK can be used by DEKs in different subjects.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "name": "my-kek",
  • "kmsType": "aws-kms",
  • "kmsKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789:key/abcd-1234",
  • "kmsProps": {
    • "region": "us-east-1"
    },
  • "doc": "Production encryption key for PII data",
  • "shared": false
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 409
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-kek",
  • "kmsType": "aws-kms",
  • "kmsKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789:key/abcd-1234",
  • "kmsProps": {
    • "region": "us-east-1"
    },
  • "doc": "Production encryption key for PII data",
  • "shared": false,
  • "ts": 1708444800000,
  • "deleted": false
}

Get a KEK

Returns the full details of the specified Key Encryption Key (KEK), including its name, KMS type, KMS key ID, properties, documentation, shared flag, and timestamps. Soft-deleted KEKs are returned only when the deleted query parameter is set to true.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns the KEK even if it has been soft-deleted.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-kek",
  • "kmsType": "aws-kms",
  • "kmsKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789:key/abcd-1234",
  • "kmsProps": {
    • "region": "us-east-1"
    },
  • "doc": "Production encryption key for PII data",
  • "shared": false,
  • "ts": 1708444800000,
  • "deleted": false
}

Update a KEK

Updates the mutable properties of the specified Key Encryption Key (KEK). Only the kmsProps, doc, and shared fields can be updated. The KEK name, KMS type, and KMS key ID are immutable after creation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK.

Request Body schema:
required
object

Updated properties for the KMS provider.

doc
string

Updated human-readable description for this KEK.

shared
boolean

Whether this KEK is shared across multiple schema subjects.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "kmsProps": {
    • "region": "us-west-2"
    },
  • "doc": "Updated production encryption key description",
  • "shared": true
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-kek",
  • "kmsType": "aws-kms",
  • "kmsKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789:key/abcd-1234",
  • "kmsProps": {
    • "region": "us-east-1"
    },
  • "doc": "Production encryption key for PII data",
  • "shared": false,
  • "ts": 1708444800000,
  • "deleted": false
}

Delete a KEK

Deletes the specified Key Encryption Key (KEK). By default this performs a soft-delete. To permanently remove the KEK, set permanent=true. A KEK MUST be soft-deleted before it can be permanently deleted. Deleting a KEK does not automatically delete its associated DEKs.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK.

query Parameters
permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the KEK from storage. The KEK MUST have been soft-deleted first.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}

Response samples

  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "error_code": 40470,
  • "message": "KEK 'my-kek' not found"
}

Undelete a KEK

Restores a previously soft-deleted Key Encryption Key (KEK). The KEK MUST currently be in a soft-deleted state for this operation to succeed.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK to restore.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}/undelete

Response samples

  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "error_code": 40470,
  • "message": "KEK 'my-kek' not found"
}

Test KEK connectivity

Validates KMS connectivity for the specified KEK by performing a round-trip encrypt/decrypt test. This is useful for verifying that the KMS provider is properly configured and accessible. The test encrypts a test payload using the KEK's KMS key, then decrypts it and verifies the result matches.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK to test.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}/test

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "name": "my-kek",
  • "kmsType": "aws-kms",
  • "kmsKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789:key/abcd-1234",
  • "kmsProps": {
    • "region": "us-east-1"
    },
  • "doc": "Production encryption key for PII data",
  • "shared": false,
  • "ts": 1708444800000,
  • "deleted": false
}

List DEK subjects

Returns a list of all DEK subject names registered under the specified KEK. The response is an array of strings. Soft-deleted DEKs are excluded unless the deleted query parameter is set to true.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK.

query Parameters
deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, soft-deleted DEK subjects are included in the results.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}/deks

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
[
  • "my-topic-value",
  • "other-topic-value"
]

Create a DEK

Creates a new Data Encryption Key (DEK) under the specified KEK for the given subject. The algorithm specifies the encryption algorithm (e.g. AES256_GCM, AES128_GCM, AES256_SIV). If encryptedKeyMaterial is provided, it is stored as-is. If omitted, the registry generates a new DEK and encrypts it using the KEK.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK under which to create the DEK.

Request Body schema:
required
subject
required
string

The subject name for this DEK. Typically corresponds to a Kafka topic.

version
integer

The version number for this DEK. If omitted, defaults to 1 or the next available version.

algorithm
string
Default: "AES256_GCM"
Enum: "AES256_GCM" "AES128_GCM" "AES256_SIV"

The encryption algorithm to use (e.g. AES256_GCM, AES128_GCM, AES256_SIV). Defaults to AES256_GCM if omitted.

encryptedKeyMaterial
string

The pre-encrypted key material. If provided, it is stored as-is. If omitted, the registry generates a new DEK and encrypts it using the KEK.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}/deks

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "subject": "my-topic-value",
  • "version": 1,
  • "algorithm": "AES256_GCM",
  • "encryptedKeyMaterial": "base64-encoded-encrypted-key-material"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 409
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "kekName": "my-kek",
  • "subject": "my-topic-value",
  • "version": 1,
  • "algorithm": "AES256_GCM",
  • "encryptedKeyMaterial": "base64-encoded-encrypted-key-material",
  • "keyMaterial": "base64-encoded-decrypted-key-material",
  • "ts": 1708444800000,
  • "deleted": false
}

Get latest DEK for a subject

Returns the latest version of the Data Encryption Key (DEK) for the specified subject under the given KEK. The optional algorithm query parameter filters by encryption algorithm. Soft-deleted DEKs are returned only when the deleted query parameter is set to true.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK.

subject
required
string

The DEK subject name.

query Parameters
algorithm
string

Filter by encryption algorithm (e.g. AES256_GCM, AES128_GCM, AES256_SIV).

deleted
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, returns the DEK even if it has been soft-deleted.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}/deks/{subject}

{

  • "algorithm": "AES256_GCM",
  • "encryptedKeyMaterial": "base64-encoded-encrypted-key-material",
  • "keyMaterial": "base64-encoded-decrypted-key-material",
  • "ts": 1708444800000,
  • "deleted": false
}

Delete a DEK

Deletes the Data Encryption Key (DEK) for the specified subject under the given KEK. By default this performs a soft-delete. To permanently remove the DEK, set permanent=true. A DEK MUST be soft-deleted before it can be permanently deleted.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK.

subject
required
string

The DEK subject name.

query Parameters
algorithm
string

Filter by encryption algorithm (e.g. AES256_GCM, AES128_GCM, AES256_SIV).

permanent
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, permanently removes the DEK from storage. The DEK MUST have been soft-deleted first.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}/deks/{subject}

Response samples

  • 404
  • 422

Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
Example

  • "error_code": 40470,
  • "message": "KEK 'my-kek' not found"
}

Create a DEK with subject in path

Creates a new Data Encryption Key (DEK) under the specified KEK for the subject specified in the URL path. This is the preferred variant of DEK creation where the subject is explicit in the path rather than the request body. When the rewrap query parameter is set to true, the existing DEK's encrypted key material is re-encrypted under the current KEK key version (used after KEK rotation).

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
name
required
string

The name of the KEK under which to create the DEK.

subject
required
string

The DEK subject name.

query Parameters
rewrap
boolean
Default: false

When set to true, re-encrypts the existing DEK's key material under the current KEK key version instead of creating a new DEK. Used after KEK rotation.

Request Body schema:
optional
version
integer

The version number for this DEK. If omitted, defaults to 1 or the next available version.

algorithm
string
Enum: "AES256_GCM" "AES128_GCM" "AES256_SIV"

The encryption algorithm (e.g. AES256_GCM, AES128_GCM, AES256_SIV). Defaults to AES256_GCM if omitted.

encryptedKeyMaterial
string

Base64-encoded encrypted key material. If omitted and the KEK is shared with a KMS provider, the registry generates key material automatically.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/dek-registry/v1/keks/{name}/deks/{subject}

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
{
  • "version": 0,
  • "algorithm": "AES256_GCM",
  • "encryptedKeyMaterial": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 409
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
{
  • "kekName": "my-kek",
  • "subject": "my-topic-value",
  • "version": 1,
  • "algorithm": "AES256_GCM",
  • "encryptedKeyMaterial": "base64-encoded-encrypted-key-material",
  • "keyMaterial": "base64-encoded-decrypted-key-material",
  • "ts": 1708444800000,
  • "deleted": false
}

Undelete a specific DEK version

Restores a previously soft-deleted Data Encryption Key (DEK) version for the specified subject under the given KEK. The DEK version MUST currently be in a soft-deleted state for this operation to succeed.

name
required
string

The name of the KEK.

subject
required
string

The DEK subject name.

version
required
integer >= 1

The DEK version number to restore. MUST be a positive integer.

query Parameters
algorithm
string

Filter by encryption algorithm (e.g. AES256_GCM, AES128_GCM, AES256_SIV).

Responses

application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json
Example

  • "error_code": 40470,
  • "message": "KEK 'my-kek' not found"
}

Analysis

Schema analysis endpoints for validation, normalization, quality scoring, field search, similarity detection, compatibility suggestions, and registry-wide statistics. These endpoints are designed primarily to support AI-assisted schema management workflows via the MCP server, and are also available as a REST API for programmatic access, CI/CD pipelines, and custom tooling. All analysis operations are read-only and do not modify registry state.

Validate a schema

Parses and validates a schema string without registering it. Returns whether the schema is syntactically and structurally valid for the given schemaType. Use this endpoint to pre-validate schemas before registration.

Validation rules by schema type:

  • Avro: Parses the JSON string using the Avro specification. Validates record structure, field types, enum symbols (MUST be non-empty), fixed sizes (MUST be > 0), default values (MUST match the field type), union syntax, and named type references. Uses the github.com/hamba/avro/v2 library.
  • Protobuf: Compiles the .proto source using github.com/bufbuild/protocompile. Validates message definitions, field numbering (MUST be unique), import resolution, service definitions, and proto2/proto3 syntax rules.
  • JSON Schema: Parses against JSON Schema Draft-07 using github.com/santhosh-tekuri/jsonschema/v5. Validates $ref resolution, type keywords, properties, required arrays, and boolean root schemas (true/false).

When the schema is valid, is_valid is true and error is empty. When invalid, is_valid is false and error contains the parse error message from the underlying library. The response always includes the detected schema_type.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
schema
required
string

The raw schema string to validate.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The schema type. Defaults to AVRO if omitted.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/validate

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "is_valid": true,
  • "schema_type": "string",
  • "error": "string"
}

Normalize a schema

Parses a schema and returns its canonical (normalized) form along with a SHA-256 content fingerprint. The canonical form removes formatting differences and produces a deterministic representation suitable for deduplication.

Normalization by schema type:

  • Avro: Applies the Avro Parsing Canonical Form (PCF), which strips doc, aliases, order, and default fields. Named types are replaced with their fully-qualified names on second reference. JSON keys are sorted deterministically.
  • Protobuf: Compiles and re-serializes the .proto source, normalizing whitespace, comment removal, and import ordering.
  • JSON Schema: Parses the JSON and re-serializes with deterministic key ordering (per RFC 8259, JSON objects are unordered, so {"type":"object","properties":...} and {"properties":...,"type":"object"} normalize to the same canonical form).

The fingerprint is a SHA-256 hash of the canonical form, hex-encoded. Two schemas with the same fingerprint are semantically identical and will share a single global ID in the registry.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
schema
required
string

The raw schema string to normalize.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The schema type.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/normalize

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 422
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "schema_type": "string",
  • "canonical": "string",
  • "fingerprint": "string"
}

Search schemas by content

Searches across all subjects for schemas whose content matches a query string or regular expression. Returns matching subject-version pairs. Useful for finding schemas that reference a particular type or contain specific field definitions.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
query
required
string

The search query (plain text or regex pattern).

regex
boolean
Default: false

If true, treat query as a regular expression.

limit
integer
Default: 50

Maximum number of results to return.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/search

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "query": "string",
  • "regex": false,
  • "limit": 50
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "query": "string",
  • "count": 0,
  • "matches": [
    • {
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "schema_type": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Find schemas by field name

Searches across all subjects for schemas containing a specific field. Fields are extracted by parsing each schema: Avro records are walked recursively (including nested records, arrays, maps, and unions), JSON Schema properties are extracted (including nested objects), and Protobuf messages are parsed for field declarations. Nested fields use dot-notation paths (e.g., address.street).

Matching modes:

  • exact (default): Generates naming convention variants of the query (snake_case, camelCase, PascalCase, kebab-case) and matches any variant case-insensitively. For example, searching for userName also matches user_name, UserName, and user-name. Matched fields return a score of 1.0.
  • fuzzy: Computes a similarity score using Levenshtein edit distance, normalized to a 0.0–1.0 range (1.0 - editDistance / maxLength). Only fields with a score at or above threshold (default 0.6) are returned. Comparison is case-insensitive.
  • regex: Compiles the query as a Go regular expression and matches against each field name. Returns a 400 error if the regex is invalid.
Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
field
required
string

The field name to search for.

mode
string
Default: "exact"
Enum: "exact" "fuzzy" "regex"

The matching mode.

threshold
number
Default: 0.6

Minimum similarity score for fuzzy matching (0.0 to 1.0).

limit
integer
Default: 50

Maximum number of results.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/search/field

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "field": "string",
  • "mode": "exact",
  • "threshold": 0.6,
  • "limit": 50
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "field": "string",
  • "mode": "string",
  • "count": 0,
  • "matches": [
    • {
      • "subject": "string",
      • "field_name": "string",
      • "field_type": "string",
      • "field_path": "string",
      • "score": 0,
      • "schema_type": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Find schemas by field type

Searches across all subjects for schemas containing fields of a given type. Useful for finding all schemas that use a specific custom type, logical type, or primitive type across the registry.

Field types are extracted from parsed schemas: Avro field types include primitives (string, int, long, boolean, etc.), named types (e.g., com.example.Address), logical types, and union representations. JSON Schema types come from the type keyword. Protobuf types include scalar types (int32, string, etc.) and message type references.

When regex is false (default), the type name is matched exactly (case-insensitive). When regex is true, the pattern is compiled as a Go regular expression and matched against each field's type string, allowing patterns like ^int(32|64)$ or .*Timestamp.*.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
type_pattern
required
string

The type name or pattern to search for.

regex
boolean
Default: false

If true, treat type_pattern as a regular expression.

limit
integer
Default: 50

Maximum number of results.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/search/type

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "type_pattern": "string",
  • "regex": false,
  • "limit": 50
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "type_pattern": "string",
  • "count": 0,
  • "matches": [
    • {
      • "subject": "string",
      • "field_name": "string",
      • "field_type": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Find similar schemas

Finds schemas that are structurally similar to a given subject's latest schema by comparing field name sets. Useful for detecting duplicate or near-duplicate schemas across subjects, identifying candidates for schema consolidation, or finding schemas that model related domain entities.

Similarity is computed using the Jaccard similarity coefficient: |intersection| / |union| of the two schemas' field name sets. Field names are normalized to snake_case before comparison so that userName and user_name are treated as the same field. A score of 1.0 means identical field sets; 0.0 means no fields in common. The response includes the list of shared_fields for each match.

The default threshold of 0.3 returns schemas sharing at least 30% of their field names. Increase to 0.8+ to find near-duplicates only.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
subject
required
string

The source subject to compare against.

threshold
number
Default: 0.3

Minimum Jaccard similarity score (0.0 to 1.0).

limit
integer
Default: 10

Maximum number of results.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/similar

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "threshold": 0.3,
  • "limit": 10
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "count": 0,
  • "similar": [
    • {
      • "subject": "string",
      • "similarity": 0,
      • "shared_fields": [
        • "string"
        ]
      }
    ]
}

Score schema quality

Scores a schema's quality across four weighted categories, producing an overall score from 0 to 100 with a letter grade. Provide either a raw schema string or a subject name to score the latest version.

Scoring categories (25 points each, 100 total):

  1. Naming (25 pts) — Checks whether field names follow snake_case convention. Fields containing uppercase letters, hyphens, or spaces are flagged. Score is proportional to the fraction of well-named fields: 25 × (good / total). This convention aligns with the Avro specification's recommendation and is the most common field naming convention across Avro, Protobuf, and JSON Schema.

  2. Documentation (25 pts) — Counts the fraction of fields that have a doc string (Avro), comment (Protobuf), or description (JSON Schema). Score is 25 × (documented / total). Undocumented schemas are harder to understand and maintain.

  3. Type Safety (25 pts) — Penalizes fields that use overly generic types: string, bytes, any, or object. These types provide no semantic meaning and bypass serialization validation. Score is 25 × (specific / total). Fields using domain-specific types (e.g., com.example.EmailAddress), enums, or logical types score higher.

  4. Evolution Readiness (25 pts) — Evaluates whether the schema is prepared for safe evolution: +10 pts if any field has a default value (enables adding fields without breaking consumers), +8 pts if a namespace (Avro) or package (Protobuf) is declared (prevents naming conflicts), +7 pts if schema-level documentation exists.

Grades: A (≥90), B (≥80), C (≥70), D (≥60), F (<60).

The quick_wins array contains actionable suggestions (e.g., "Rename field userName to user_name", "Add documentation to fields").

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
schema
string

The raw schema string to score. Either schema or subject MUST be provided.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The schema type.

subject
string

Score the latest schema version of this subject.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/quality

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "subject": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "overall_score": 0,
  • "max_score": 0,
  • "grade": "string",
  • "categories": [
    • {
      • "name": "string",
      • "score": 0,
      • "max_score": 0,
      • "issues": [
        • "string"
        ]
      }
    ],
  • "quick_wins": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

Get schema complexity

Computes structural complexity metrics for a schema and assigns a complexity grade from A (simple) to D (very complex). Provide either a raw schema string or a subject name to analyze the latest version.

Metrics computed:

  • field_count — Total number of fields across all nested records/objects.
  • max_depth — Maximum nesting level. A flat record has depth 1; a record with a nested record field has depth 2, etc. Computed from the dot-notation field paths (e.g., address.street.number has depth 3).

Complexity grades:

  • A — ≤15 fields AND ≤3 nesting depth. Simple, easy to understand and maintain.
  • B — ≤30 fields AND ≤4 nesting depth. Moderate complexity, typical for most domain objects.
  • C — ≤50 fields AND ≤5 nesting depth. Complex schema that may benefit from decomposition into referenced sub-schemas.
  • D — >50 fields OR >5 nesting depth. Very complex; strongly consider breaking into smaller, referenced schemas for maintainability and reuse.
Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
schema
string

The raw schema string. Either schema or subject MUST be provided.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"
subject
string

Analyze the latest schema version of this subject.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/schemas/complexity

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "subject": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 404
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "schema_type": "string",
  • "field_count": 0,
  • "max_depth": 0,
  • "grade": "string"
}

Validate a subject name

Checks whether a subject name follows the conventions of a given naming strategy. Returns validation issues and a suggested corrected name. These strategies correspond to the Confluent Schema Registry subject name strategies used by Kafka serializers.

Naming strategies:

  • topic_name (default) — Subject MUST end with -key or -value (e.g., orders-value, users-key). This is the default Confluent TopicNameStrategy where the subject is {topic}-{key|value}.
  • record_name — Subject MUST be a valid qualified name using dot-separated identifiers (e.g., com.example.User). Each identifier MUST start with a letter or underscore, followed by letters, digits, or underscores. This matches the Confluent RecordNameStrategy where the subject is the fully-qualified schema name.
  • topic_record_name — Subject MUST contain a hyphen separating the topic and record portions (e.g., orders-com.example.Order). This matches the Confluent TopicRecordNameStrategy where the subject is {topic}-{record_name}.
Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
subject
required
string

The subject name to validate.

strategy
string
Default: "topic_name"
Enum: "topic_name" "record_name" "topic_record_name"

The naming strategy to validate against.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/validate

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "strategy": "topic_name"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "strategy": "string",
  • "valid": true,
  • "issues": [
    • "string"
    ],
  • "suggestion": "string"
}

Match subjects by pattern

Finds subjects matching a pattern. Useful for discovering subjects when the exact name is not known, or for filtering subjects by naming convention.

Matching modes:

  • regex (default) — Compiles the pattern as a Go regular expression and tests each subject name. Returns a 400 error for invalid regex.
  • glob — Matches using wildcard patterns where * matches any sequence of characters. Comparison is case-insensitive. For example, orders-* matches orders-value, orders-key, and orders-avro-value.
  • fuzzy — Computes Levenshtein-based similarity (0.0–1.0) between the pattern and each subject name. Only subjects with a score at or above threshold (default 0.6) are returned. Useful for finding subjects with typos or slight naming variations.
Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
pattern
required
string

The pattern to match subject names against.

mode
string
Default: "regex"
Enum: "regex" "glob" "fuzzy"

The matching mode.

threshold
number
Default: 0.6

Minimum similarity score for fuzzy matching (0.0 to 1.0).

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/match

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "pattern": "string",
  • "mode": "regex",
  • "threshold": 0.6
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "pattern": "string",
  • "mode": "string",
  • "count": 0,
  • "matches": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

Responses

http://localhost:8081/subjects/count

basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The subject name.

version
required
integer

The schema version number.

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version}/export

Export all versions of a subject

Exports all schema versions registered under a subject. Returns each version with its schema body, ID, and type. Useful for backup and migration.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The subject name.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/export

  • 200
  • 404
Content type
application/json

  • "count": 0,
  • "versions": [
    • {
      • "subject": "string",
      • "version": 0,
      • "id": 0,
      • "schema": "string",
      • "schema_type": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Diff two schema versions

Compares two versions of a subject's schema and returns the structural differences at the field level. If version2 is omitted, compares against the latest version.

The diff extracts fields from both schema versions and computes:

  • added — Fields present in version2 but not in version1 (new fields).
  • removed — Fields present in version1 but not in version2 (deleted fields).
  • changed — Fields present in both versions but with a different type (e.g., a field changed from string to int), showing both old_type and new_type.

Field matching uses normalized snake_case names, so userName in version1 and user_name in version2 are treated as the same field. This endpoint is read-only and does not perform compatibility checking — use POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version} for that.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The subject name.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
version1
integer
Default: 1

The first version to compare.

version2
integer

The second version to compare. Defaults to latest.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/diff

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "version1": 1,
  • "version2": 0
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "version1": 0,
  • "version2": 0,
  • "added": [
    • {
      • "field": "string",
      • "type": "string"
      }
    ],
  • "removed": [
    • {
      • "field": "string",
      • "type": "string"
      }
    ],
  • "changed": [
    • {
      • "field": "string",
      • "old_type": "string",
      • "new_type": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Suggest schema evolution

Given a subject and a set of proposed changes, provides guidance on how to evolve the schema while maintaining compatibility with the subject's configured compatibility level. Returns the subject's current version, its compatibility level, and a message describing whether the proposed changes are safe.

Each change in the changes array describes an action (add, remove, or modify), the field name, and optionally a type. The endpoint evaluates these against the compatibility rules — for example, under BACKWARD compatibility, adding a field with a default is safe, but removing a field is not.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The subject name.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
Array of objects

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/evolve

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "changes": [
    • {
      • "action": "string",
      • "field": "string",
      • "type": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "current_version": 0,
  • "compatibility_level": "string",
  • "changes_requested": 0,
  • "message": "string"
}

Plan migration path

Given a subject and a target schema, computes a step-by-step migration plan to evolve from the subject's current latest schema to the target. Each step in the plan is an individually compatible schema change that can be registered as a new version.

The planner diffs the current and target schemas (using the same field extraction as the diff endpoint), then decomposes the changes into the minimum number of safe steps based on the subject's compatibility level. For example, under BACKWARD compatibility, a migration that adds two fields and removes one might be decomposed into: (1) add field A with default, (2) add field B with default, (3) deprecate and remove field C in a separate step.

The steps array contains human-readable descriptions of each migration step. step_count is the total number of steps required.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The subject name.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
target_schema
required
string

The target schema to migrate to.

schema_type
string
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

The schema type of the target schema.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/subjects/{subject}/migrate

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "target_schema": "string",
  • "schema_type": "AVRO"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "current_version": 0,
  • "compatibility_level": "string",
  • "steps": [
    • "string"
    ],
  • "step_count": 0
}

Check compatibility against multiple subjects

Tests whether a schema is compatible with the latest version of multiple subjects simultaneously. Returns per-subject compatibility results. Useful for validating a shared schema across multiple topics.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema to test.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"
subjects
required
Array of strings

The list of subjects to check compatibility against.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/compatibility/check

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO",
  • "subjects": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "results": [
    • {
      • "subject": "string",
      • "is_compatible": true,
      • "error": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Suggest compatible changes

Returns rule-based suggestions for what kinds of schema changes are safe under the subject's current compatibility level. Helps developers understand the constraints before attempting schema evolution.

Suggestions by compatibility level:

  • BACKWARD / BACKWARD_TRANSITIVE — "Add new fields with default values", "Do NOT remove existing fields", "Do NOT change field types". New consumers can read data written by old producers.
  • FORWARD / FORWARD_TRANSITIVE — "Remove fields (new consumers will ignore them)", "Do NOT add required fields without defaults". Old consumers can read data written by new producers.
  • FULL / FULL_TRANSITIVE — "Only add optional fields with defaults", "Do NOT remove or rename fields". Both old and new consumers can read each other's data.
  • NONE — "Any change is allowed (no compatibility checks)".
Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The subject name.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/suggest

Response samples

  • 200
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "compatibility_level": "string",
  • "suggestions": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

Explain compatibility failure

Tests a schema against a subject's latest version and explains why the schema is or is not compatible. Returns the compatibility level, result, and a human-readable explanation of any failure.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
subject
required
string

The subject name.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
schema
required
string

The candidate schema to test.

schemaType
string
Default: "AVRO"
Enum: "AVRO" "PROTOBUF" "JSON"

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/compatibility/subjects/{subject}/explain

Request samples

application/json

  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "AVRO"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject": "string",
  • "compatibility_level": "string",
  • "is_compatible": true,
  • "error": "string",
  • "explanation": "string"
}

Compare two subjects

Compares the field structures of two subjects' latest schemas. Returns fields that are shared between both, unique to the first subject, or unique to the second subject. Useful for identifying field overlap and potential schema consolidation opportunities.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
Request Body schema: application/json
required
subject1
required
string

The first subject name.

subject2
required
string

The second subject name.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/compatibility/compare

Request samples

  • Payload
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject1": "string",
  • "subject2": "string"
}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 404
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject1": "string",
  • "subject2": "string",
  • "shared": [
    • "string"
    ],
  • "only_in_sub1": [
    • "string"
    ],
  • "only_in_sub2": [
    • "string"
    ]
}

Get registry statistics

Returns aggregate statistics about the registry including the total number of subjects, total number of schema versions, and a breakdown of schema types (AVRO, PROTOBUF, JSON).

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/statistics

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject_count": 0,
  • "version_count": 0,
  • "type_counts": {
    • "property1": 0,
    • "property2": 0
    }
}

Check field consistency

Checks how a specific field name is used across all schemas in the registry and reports whether its type is consistent. This is a data governance tool for detecting type drift — for example, user_id being long in one schema and string in another.

The search generates naming convention variants of the field name (snake_case, camelCase, PascalCase, kebab-case) and matches any variant against every schema's extracted fields. The response includes:

  • consistent — true if all usages of this field have the same type (i.e., type_counts has only one entry), false otherwise.
  • type_counts — A map of type name to usage count (e.g., {"long": 5, "string": 2} indicates an inconsistency).
  • usages — Each individual occurrence with its subject, matched field name, and field type.
Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth
path Parameters
field
required
string

The field name to check.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/statistics/fields/{field}

Response samples

  • 200
  • 400
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "field": "string",
  • "consistent": true,
  • "type_counts": {
    • "property1": 0,
    • "property2": 0
    },
  • "usages": [
    • {
      • "subject": "string",
      • "field_name": "string",
      • "field_type": "string"
      }
    ]
}

Detect schema patterns

Analyzes all schemas in the registry and identifies common field patterns — fields that appear in two or more subjects. Useful for discovering shared data models and candidates for schema consolidation.

Authorizations:
basicAuthapiKeybearerAuth

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/statistics/patterns

Response samples

  • 200
  • 500
Content type
application/json
{
  • "subject_count": 0,
  • "common_fields": [
    • {
      • "field": "string",
      • "count": 0
      }
    ],
  • "pattern_count": 0
}

context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

string
schemaType
string

path Parameters
context
required
string^[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+$
Example: .team-a

The schema registry context name. Contexts provide multi-tenant isolation. The name MUST include a leading dot (e.g. .team-a). If omitted, it is automatically prepended.

Request Body schema: application/json
required
schema
string
schemaType
string
subject
string

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/contexts/{context}/schemas/quality

application/json
{
  • "schema": "string",
  • "schemaType": "string",
  • "subject": "string"
}

Documentation

Endpoints for serving the interactive API documentation (Swagger UI) and the raw OpenAPI specification. Available only when the server is configured with docs_enabled.

Swagger UI

Serves the interactive Swagger UI for exploring the API. This endpoint is only available when the server is configured with docs_enabled: true. It does not require authentication.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/docs

OpenAPI specification

Returns the raw OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification for this API in YAML format. This endpoint is only available when the server is configured with docs_enabled: true. It does not require authentication.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/openapi.yaml

Prometheus metrics

Returns Prometheus-formatted metrics for the registry. This endpoint does not require authentication. Metrics include request counts, latencies, cache statistics, and storage health indicators.

Responses

Local development server

http://localhost:8081/metrics
